was the ninety season of [Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"), the top-level [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") league of [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"). Season began on 27 July 2007 and ended on 7 May 2008. [CFR Cluj](/wiki/CFR_Cluj "CFR Cluj") became champions, winning their first trophy and ending a 17-year-long reign of [Bucharest](/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest") based teams.

* [Gloria Buzău](/wiki/FC_Gloria_Buz%C4%83u "FC Gloria Buzău")   (after 20 years of absence) * [Universitatea Cluj](/wiki/FC_Universitatea_Cluj "FC Universitatea Cluj")   (after 8 years of absence) * [Dacia Mioveni](/wiki/CS_Mioveni "CS Mioveni")   (debut) |

* [Progresul București](/wiki/FC_Progresul_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Progresul București")   (ended 15-year stay) * [Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești")   (ended 13-year stay) * [Jiul Petroșani](/wiki/CS_Jiul_Petro%C8%99ani "CS Jiul Petroșani")   (ended 2-year stay) |

Buzău  [Buzău](/wiki/FC_Gloria_Buz%C4%83u "FC Gloria Buzău")  CFR  [CFR](/wiki/CFR_Cluj "CFR Cluj")  Ceahlăul  [Ceahlăul](/wiki/FC_Ceahl%C4%83ul_Piatra_Neam%C8%9B "FC Ceahlăul Piatra Neamț")  Craiova  [Craiova](/wiki/FC_U_Craiova_1948 "FC U Craiova 1948")  Dacia  [Dacia](/wiki/CS_Mioveni "CS Mioveni")  Farul  [Farul](/wiki/FCV_Farul_Constan%C8%9Ba "FCV Farul Constanța")  Gloria  [Gloria](/wiki/ACF_Gloria_Bistri%C8%9Ba "ACF Gloria Bistrița")  Oțelul  [Oțelul](/wiki/FC_O%C8%9Belul_Gala%C8%9Bi "FC Oțelul Galați")  Pandurii  [Pandurii](/wiki/CS_Pandurii_T%C3%A2rgu_Jiu "CS Pandurii Târgu Jiu")  Poli Iași  [Poli Iași](/wiki/FC_Politehnica_Ia%C8%99i_(1945) "FC Politehnica Iași (1945)")  Poli Timișoara  [Poli Timișoara](/wiki/FC_Politehnica_Timi%C8%99oara "FC Politehnica Timișoara")  Universitatea  [Universitatea](/wiki/FC_Universitatea_Cluj "FC Universitatea Cluj")  Urziceni  [Urziceni](/wiki/FC_Unirea_Urziceni "FC Unirea Urziceni")  UTA  [UTA](/wiki/FC_UTA_Arad "FC UTA Arad")  Vaslui  [Vaslui](/wiki/CS_Sporting_Vaslui "CS Sporting Vaslui")  Bucharest teams Dinamo Rapid Steaua  **[Bucharest](/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest") teams** [Dinamo](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București") [Rapid](/wiki/FC_Rapid_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Rapid București") [Steaua](/wiki/FC_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Steaua București")  2007–08 Liga I (Romania)  [2007–08 Liga I is located in Bucharest](/wiki/File:Romania_Bucharest_location_map.svg "2007–08 Liga I is located in Bucharest") Dinamo  [Dinamo](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București")  Rapid  [Rapid](/wiki/FC_Rapid_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Rapid București")  Steaua  [Steaua](/wiki/FC_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Steaua București")  Location of Bucharest teams. | | [Rapid București](/wiki/FC_Rapid_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Rapid București") |

Gloria Bistrița was relocated to [Municipal Stadium](/wiki/Stadionul_Municipal_(Reghin) "Stadionul Municipal (Reghin)") from [Reghin](/wiki/Reghin "Reghin") for the second part of this season because [Gloria Stadium](/wiki/Stadionul_Jean_P%C4%83dureanu "Stadionul Jean Pădureanu") was under renovations.
Capacity of Ion Moina Stadium has been reduced from 28,000 to 7,600 due to advanced degradation of the resistance structure.

The **2007–08 Liga I** was the ninety season of [Liga I](/wiki/Liga_I "Liga I"), the top-level [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") league of [Romania](/wiki/Romania "Romania"). Season began on 27 July 2007 and ended on 7 May 2008. [CFR Cluj](/wiki/CFR_Cluj "CFR Cluj") became champions, winning their first trophy and ending a 17-year-long reign of [Bucharest](/wiki/Bucharest "Bucharest") based teams.

[CFR Cluj](/wiki/CFR_Cluj "CFR Cluj") will play in the [Champions League](/wiki/UEFA_Champions_League "UEFA Champions League") [group stage](/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_UEFA_Champions_League#Group_stage "2008–09 UEFA Champions League"), while [Steaua București](/wiki/FC_Steaua_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Steaua București") qualified for the Champions League [third qualifying round](/wiki/2008%E2%80%9309_UEFA_Champions_League#Third_qualifying_round "2008–09 UEFA Champions League"). [Rapid București](/wiki/FC_Rapid_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Rapid București"), [Dinamo București](/wiki/FC_Dinamo_Bucure%C8%99ti "FC Dinamo București"), [Unirea Urziceni](/wiki/FC_Unirea_Urziceni "FC Unirea Urziceni") and [Politehnica Știința Timișoara](/wiki/FC_Politehnica_1921_%C8%98tiin%C8%9Ba_Timi%C8%99oara "FC Politehnica 1921 Știința Timișoara") qualified for the [UEFA Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Cup "UEFA Cup") [first round](/wiki/UEFA_Cup_2008%E2%80%9309#First_round "UEFA Cup 2008–09"). The highest placed team that has not qualified for the UEFA Cup is allowed the opportunity to compete in the third round of the [UEFA Intertoto Cup](/wiki/UEFA_Intertoto_Cup "UEFA Intertoto Cup"), provided they have applied to enter the Intertoto Cup in the next season. [Vaslui](/wiki/CS_Sporting_Vaslui "CS Sporting Vaslui"), [Oțelul Galați](/wiki/O%C8%9Belul_Gala%C8%9Bi "Oțelul Galați") and [Gloria Bistrița](/wiki/ACF_Gloria_Bistri%C8%9Ba "ACF Gloria Bistrița") are the only teams that have applied,[[2]](#cite_note-2) with [Vaslui](/wiki/CS_Sporting_Vaslui "CS Sporting Vaslui") securing their participation.
